([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 블랙아이드필승; lit. '"Black Eyed Victory"') were a South Korean [music production](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") and songwriting duo made up of Rado (Song Joo-young) and Choi Kyu-sung, formed in 2014.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) They produced hit [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") songs such as [Sistar](/wiki/Sistar "Sistar")'s "[Touch My Body](/wiki/Touch_N_Move "Touch N Move")"; [Miss A](/wiki/Miss_A "Miss A")'s "[Only You](/wiki/Only_You_(Miss_A_song) "Only You (Miss A song)")"; [Twice](/wiki/Twice "Twice")'s "[Like Ooh-Ahh](/wiki/Like_Ooh-Ahh "Like Ooh-Ahh")", "[Cheer Up](/wiki/Cheer_Up_(song) "Cheer Up (song)")", "[TT](/wiki/TT_(song) "TT (song)")", "[Likey](/wiki/Likey "Likey")", and "[Fancy](/wiki/Fancy_(Twice_song) "Fancy (Twice song)")"; and [Apink](/wiki/Apink "Apink")'s "[I'm So Sick](/wiki/One_%26_Six "One & Six")", "[Eung Eung](/wiki/Percent_(EP) "Percent (EP)")", and "[Dumhdurum](/wiki/Look_(EP) "Look (EP)")".[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

South Korean producer duo

Not to be confused with [Black Eyed Peas](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Peas "Black Eyed Peas").

| Black Eyed Pilseung 블랙아이드필승 | |
| --- | --- |
| Also known as | Black Eyed Victory |
| Origin | South Korea |
| Genres | [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") |
| Years active | 2014–2025 |
| Label | [High Up Entertainment](/wiki/High_Up_Entertainment "High Up Entertainment") |
|  | |
| Past members | * Rado (Song Joo-young) * Choi Kyu-sung |
|  | |
| Website | [highup-ent.com/en/bep](http://www.highup-ent.com/en/bep/) |

**Black Eyed Pilseung** ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 블랙아이드필승; lit. '"Black Eyed Victory"') were a South Korean [music production](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") and songwriting duo made up of Rado (Song Joo-young) and Choi Kyu-sung, formed in 2014.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) They produced hit [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") songs such as [Sistar](/wiki/Sistar "Sistar")'s "[Touch My Body](/wiki/Touch_N_Move "Touch N Move")"; [Miss A](/wiki/Miss_A "Miss A")'s "[Only You](/wiki/Only_You_(Miss_A_song) "Only You (Miss A song)")"; [Twice](/wiki/Twice "Twice")'s "[Like Ooh-Ahh](/wiki/Like_Ooh-Ahh "Like Ooh-Ahh")", "[Cheer Up](/wiki/Cheer_Up_(song) "Cheer Up (song)")", "[TT](/wiki/TT_(song) "TT (song)")", "[Likey](/wiki/Likey "Likey")", and "[Fancy](/wiki/Fancy_(Twice_song) "Fancy (Twice song)")"; and [Apink](/wiki/Apink "Apink")'s "[I'm So Sick](/wiki/One_%26_Six "One & Six")", "[Eung Eung](/wiki/Percent_(EP) "Percent (EP)")", and "[Dumhdurum](/wiki/Look_(EP) "Look (EP)")".[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Before the duo's formation, both members often collaborated with [Shinsadong Tiger](/wiki/Shinsadong_Tiger "Shinsadong Tiger"). Rado worked on hit songs such as [4Minute](/wiki/4Minute "4Minute")'s "[Volume Up](/wiki/Volume_Up_(EP) "Volume Up (EP)")", [Apink](/wiki/Apink "Apink")'s "[My My](/wiki/Snow_Pink "Snow Pink")" and "[Hush](/wiki/Une_Ann%C3%A9e "Une Année")", and [Trouble Maker](/wiki/Trouble_Maker_(duo) "Trouble Maker (duo)")'s "[Trouble Maker](/wiki/Trouble_Maker_(EP) "Trouble Maker (EP)")" and "[Now](/wiki/Chemistry_(Trouble_Maker_EP) "Chemistry (Trouble Maker EP)")", while Choi Kyu-sung was involved in [T-ara](/wiki/T-ara "T-ara")'s "[Roly-Poly](/wiki/Roly-Poly_(T-ara_song) "Roly-Poly (T-ara song)")", [Hyuna](/wiki/Hyuna "Hyuna")'s "[Bubble Pop](/wiki/Bubble_Pop "Bubble Pop")", and [Beast](/wiki/Beast_(South_Korean_band) "Beast (South Korean band)")'s "[Fiction](/wiki/Fiction_and_Fact "Fiction and Fact")".

In August 2017, the duo joined forces with [CJ E&M](/wiki/CJ_E%26M "CJ E&M") to launch a new entertainment company called [High Up Entertainment](/wiki/High_Up_Entertainment "High Up Entertainment"),[[7]](#cite_note-Hankook_Ilbo-7) which became an independent company in 2018. In February 2019, the duo debuted their first boy duo 415 ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 사이로).[[8]](#cite_note-8) In November 2020, the duo debuted their first girl group [STAYC](/wiki/STAYC "STAYC") ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 스테이씨).[[9]](#cite_note-9) In March 2025, Rado and Choi Kyu-sung parted ways due to differing musical styles, with Choi leaving High Up Entertainment.[[7]](#cite_note-Hankook_Ilbo-7)

## Members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Black_Eyed_Pilseung&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Members")]

### Rado

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Black_Eyed_Pilseung&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Rado")]

Rado ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 라도; born Song Joo-young [송주영; *Song Juyeong*]) was born on July 11, 1984.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-Daum-11) Prior to being a songwriter and music producer, he was a member of the now-defunct boy band Someday.[[11]](#cite_note-Daum-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) He was also known for collaborating with rapper [Dok2](/wiki/Dok2 "Dok2"), starting with the 2010 [EP album](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") *It's We*[[13]](#cite_note-13) and songs "Girl Girl" (together with [The Quiett](/wiki/The_Quiett "The Quiett"), from *Thunderground Mixtape Vol. 2*) and "Baby Let's Go" (together with The Quiett and B-Free, from *Rapsolute Mixtape, Vol. 1*), then the 2011 songs "Break Beatz" (together with [Double K](/wiki/Illson "Illson"), from *Flow 2 Flow*), "That's Me" (from *Hustle Real Hard*) and "Come Closer/Flow2nite" (together with The Quiett, also from *Hustle Real Hard*), and the 2012 song "Love & Life" (from *[Love & Life, The Album](/wiki/Love_%26_Life,_The_Album "Love & Life, The Album")*).

### Choi Kyu-sung

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Black_Eyed_Pilseung&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Choi Kyu-sung")]

Choi Kyu-sung ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 최규성; *Choe Gyuseong*) was born on May 7, 1984, in [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul").[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
